records.ety: [F. enchanter, L. incantare to chant or utter a magic formula over or against one, to bewitch; in in, against + cantare to sing. See Chant, and cf. Incantation.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Enchanted; p. pr. & vb. n. Enchanting.]

defs: To charm by sorcery; to act on by enchantment; to get control of by magical words and rites.
quotes: And now about the caldron sing, Like elves and fairies in a ring, Enchanting all that you put in.

defs: To delight in a high degree; to charm; to enrapture; as, music enchants the ear.
quotes: Arcadia was the charmed circle where all his spirits forever should be enchanted.
